1408.name: Oldgate Road
1408.description: The gnarled trees which line the road have a twisted, malignant look to them and seem to look accusingly at you as you pass by. Even the birds and insects have grown silent as if waiting for you to begone before they continue their business.

npcs.name: Keen-eyed xymara
npcs.description: face, absorbing every detail.  It flicks its reptilian tongue in a silent warning, and then returns to its observation of the surroundings.

1409.name: Oldgate Road
1409.description: A large, formidable gate can be seen in the distance to the south, regulating passage into what looks to be a rather seedy city. A wide trail leads north toward an ancient drawbridge. A thin trail leads through a grassy field to the east, and Oldgate Road continues into the distance to the west.

2828.name: Newbills Barbery
2828.description: This is a typical barber shop. A comfortable chair, razors, soap, hot towels, pliers for pulling teeth, lances for boils, leaches in a small aquarium, bloody sawdust swept into a corner, and dirty jokes.

2829.name: West Goldfish Lane
2829.description: Someone actually spotted a goldfish here a few years ago. At least that's the rumor. Judging by the smell, though, the goldfish was already dead.

2072.name: East Goldfish Lane
2072.description: The public restroom to the south smells better than the Brown River to the north. Not only does the Brown River smell bad, but it looks as though if it weren't so slippery it would support a lot of weight.

2073.name: East Goldfish Lane
2073.description: The breeze tries heroically to ruffle the water of the Brown River, but the water is just too slick.

2074.name: Lindas Curve
2074.description: Small flowerbeds line this gently curving street. It is one of the few places in this quarter that you might like to spend some time in.

2840.name: Sticky Alley
2840.description: A narrow, inset door with a greasy handle leads north, while the alley works its way east and west.

2076.name: Bailey Street
2076.description: This is getting to be a bad part of town again. People hurry by without meeting your eyes. It might be a good time to check your purse. Lindas Curve offers some hope to the north, or you can take your chances to the east or west.

2077.name: Bailey Street
2077.description: A low, arched entryway nearly hides an exit to the south. You wonder what mysterious secrets might be hiding there.

2078.name: Jenny Alley
2078.description: This is a perfect alley: narrow and tight. A large carving on the east wall draws your eye. You can go north or west.

2079.name: Jenny Alley
2079.description: A heavy iron grate in the ground is secured by a heavy padlock.

2080.name: Cypress Gardens Way
2080.description: If it weren't for all of the colorful penmanship that illuminates the city wall here, this would be a pretty boring place.

2081.name: Cypress Gardens Way
2081.description: The city wall casts a shadow on this street through most of the day.

2082.name: East Ironbar Street
2082.description: The wall of this mysterious building presses against the sidewalk to the north until it is stopped by Cypress Gardens Way. To the south is a large construction area.

2083.name: East Ironbar Street
2083.description: A windowless brick building lines the street to the north, while, to the south, temple street gracefully leads away. Ironbar Street continues to the east and west. A prostitute goes south.

2720.name: Gypsy Camp
2720.description: You have come upon a secluded campsite, screened from observation from without by natural and artificial walls of brush. It is a rather small and pitiful little camp with a single tent to the east.

npcs.name: Gypsy songstress
npcs.description: Clad in a simple dress of white and red, and adorned with glass beads and gaudy jewelery, she skips carelessly down the road humming softly to

npcs.name: Serious looking gypsy
npcs.description: He doesn't look as shiftless and untrustworthy of his fellows, but his smell still makes you want to keep your distance.

2084.name: Temple Street
2084.description: To the north, the street lets out on Ironbar Street. To the south it continues around the side of a small temple. The clink and clack of coins can be heard from the brick buildings to the east and west.

npcs.name: Piougnatious
npcs.description: You're not sure if it's a man or a woman.  It's wearing a long robe and has medium-length, curled hair.  It may be a wig.  It's hard to tell.

2087.name: Outside of Surtower
2087.description: The dilapidated southern entrance to Oldgate lies before you, clothed in rust and mortar. Amber grass covers the land to the south and east, while to the west rise numerous small bushes which quickly give way to trees. You can travel eastward along the wall or enter Oldgate to the north.

npcs.name: Aislinn
npcs.description: It was summoned by Silaetha to keep Oldgate's citizens from wandering into certain death.  My how the neighborhood has gone downhill.

2856.name: The Witchery
2856.description: This establishment is decorated with lacy taste. The curtains, like most of the fabric in the room, are made of printed cotton with a blue and white checkered pattern. There are also lots of stylized depictions of geese and ducks wearing bonnets and carrying wicker baskets. The decor give you the overall impression of a padded cell.

npcs.name: Minion of the Four
npcs.description: A mist fills the hexagram, and it pulses through every color that you can see, and some that you cannot.  Deep in this swirling cylinder is the shifting outline of a powerful woman.  She smells like the mist from wavecrests.

npcs.name: Silaetha
npcs.description: She is a tiny, frail woman, who moves fast enough that she always seems in danger of shattering herself against a counter or wall.

2857.name: Triangle Room
2857.description: This triangular room has a small bed in one corner, and a table holding the paraphernalia of magic.

npcs.name: Chipmunk
npcs.description: There is nothing unusual about her.  Except, perhaps, her business-like air. Or maybe it's that she is floating about a foot off the ground.  No.  No.  It is definitely that she is glowing with a strange light.

2849.name: Wellway Way
2849.description: A sign to the east reads "Guild's Gym: Member's Only. " In small letters underneath you see the legend "No questions asked. " This looks like a tough place.

2859.name: Cat Alley
2859.description: Small, feline footprints mark the mud in this narrow way. Another alley goes southwest, and an iron door is set in the wall to the northeast. The alley continues.

2854.name: West Ironbar Street
2854.description: Looking north, you can see all the way to the Brown River. To the east the neighborhood looks better, but if you go west you'd better oil your scabbards.

2855.name: West Ironbar Street
2855.description: A foul smell from the south warns you of a small magic shop. Another street leads to the north, or you can continue either east or west.

2858.name: West Ironbar Street
2858.description: Guard Street twists its way north of here, while a small alley opens directly to the south. West Ironbar continues east and west from here.

2862.name: West Ironbar Street
2862.description: The local commissariat is to the north, and across the street to the south is a pet shop painted in a dull green.

2861.name: Xantias Exotics
2861.description: These walls surround an assortment of wicker, steel, and wooden cages, and glass aquariums. You enjoy the rich smells of animal fodder and waste. A large steel door is set into the southwestern wall.

2860.name: Xantias Stockroom
2860.description: This place is STUFFED with kitten chow.

1723.name: Oldgate Road
1723.description: You move along a short stretch of road just north of Oldgate. The road continues a little ways to the north then turns west and continues out of sight.

npcs.name: Dockworker
npcs.description: on the color of the river, adapting to his unfortunate environment.

1724.name: Outside Oldgate
1724.description: This gate used to protect the city from foreign invaders. Now it protects the countryside from criminals. The gate passes through a crenelated tower where guards keep an eye on the traffic. A narrow, exposed stairway climbs up the structure.

npcs.name: Alley cat
npcs.description: with him for the local rats.  Perhaps some of the more desperate locals do.

1726.name: Auldbridge
1726.description: The Brown River flows beneath this bridge. As you look down you see how it got its name. A dip would be good for your skin, but bad for your immune system. The multicolored iridescence of the surface oil is beautiful to watch. To the north you see the Oldgate, which someone has been audacious enough to adorn with graffiti, and to the south lies Main Street. A large brown rat scurries south.

1727.name: Main Avenue
1727.description: Wayfarers wend their way through the potholes on this cobblestoned street which leads north and south, or they can try their luck and their patience on Goldfish Avenue.

1728.name: Main Avenue
1728.description: The cobblestones of Main Street extend to the north and south. Bailey Street lies to the east, and to the west you can see a pole painted in a red and white spiral which advertises that the doorway next to it belongs to a barber.

1729.name: Main Avenue
1729.description: Pigeons and vandals have effaced the statue of a forgotten defender of the city, but he still looks vigilantly to the defense of Auldgate, far to the north. Ironbar Street intersects with Main Avenue here.

1730.name: Main Avenue
1730.description: Main Avenue bisects Poortown. To the east is Temple Street, and to the west is the opening of an alley.

1731.name: Main Avenue
1731.description: The way widens, and you can travel north, south, or west in this dilapidated section of town. Surtower is directly to the south. A dump can be found to the west.

npcs.name: Obsidian statue
npcs.description: Beneath its stoney black hood lies the carved visage of a skeleton.

2501.name: East Ironbar Street
2501.description: The main entrance to the old district jail is northward. Ragged children move in small swarms around the large marble steps leading there. The bars that gave the street its name sprout from the rough, heavy, stone walls.

1732.name: Surtower
1732.description: This tower is a companion to the one several blocks to the north. It is, however, in a miserable state of repair. It looks like the only way to close the portcullis would be to break the chain that is wrapped around the rusting iron chain-wheel. Stairs lead upward, and the gates lead north and south.

2505.name: Practice Auditorium
2505.description: A small raised stage allows the master to observe his students from every angle. A trap-door leads into murky darkness under the stage.

npcs.name: Lefty
npcs.description: is always dealing with volitile tempers.  His left hand is missing and the stump has been adorned with a tatoo of a large and bloodshot eye.

2506.name: Bailey Street
2506.description: The public restrooms are north of here. It's a good thing that you don't have to use them, because you'd have to push your way through a thick swarm of flies.

2761.name: Short Street
2761.description: This is an appropriate name. You can go north or south.

1734.name: Temple Street
1734.description: The road winds both north and west, but the most interesting feature of this section of street is the small, domed temple. Two interlocking circles are carved in the lintel, and they are surrounded by a repeating pattern of pluses and minuses. To the east is the temple stair. A large brown rat scurries north.

e.to_zone: Oldtown Temple District - City of Oldgate

2075.name: The Arcade
2075.description: Wares are laid out on small blankets in this covered street. Mr Ghanji keeps a sharp eye on them because he works on commission.

npcs.name: Mr. Ghanji
npcs.description: He is a small man with dark brown skin and red-rimmed eyes.  His eyes return your gaze with a side-long appraisal.

2504.name: Thieves Guild Entrance Hall
2504.description: A small table and bench are carved out of one block of stone that is cemented into the floor. A mural of St. Nicholas spruces up the place a bit. Opposite the mural is a staircase leading upward, and to the east, opposite the main entrance, is a doorway.

npcs.name: Badger
npcs.description: He could cut your purse or your throat.  It's all the same to him.

2842.name: Water Buffalo Way
2842.description: A narrow, dim alley to the east leads to no good. Your mother warned you about these places.

2843.name: Water Buffalo Way
2843.description: Red light is cast from the upstairs windows of the building to the east, but there seems to be no way in from this street. You can continue north or south.

2844.name: West Goldfish Lane
2844.description: It doesn't seem as though there is a lot of gold here. There certainly aren't a lot of fish. The top of a flight of crumbling, stone stairs beckons to you from the north.

2845.name: Wellway Way
2845.description: This is as far as you can go to the west unless you intend to climb over or dig under the city wall. You can go south, east, or, if you're feeling suicidal, you can jump in the brown river. But if you really want to kill yourself, it would probably be more pleasant to die by falling on your sword.

2846.name: Wellway Way
2846.description: It's good to be away from the stench of the river. On the other hand, this means that you are closer to the stench of the city. You can go north, toward the river, or south, toward danger.

2847.name: Wellway Way
2847.description: This spot is far enough away from the Brown River that weeds can grow here and there. The road continues to the north and south, and a wide opening between the arms of a building leads east.

2848.name: Well Court
2848.description: This area is a center of activity in this section of town because it is the only public place with potable water.

1406.name: Oldgate Road
1406.description: You move along what was once a cobbled road but the ravages of time and lack of repairs have reduced it to a weed-grown cart path. To the north you can see where the road intersects another, better maintained road. To the south, Oldgate Road continues.

1407.name: Oldgate Road
1407.description: The encroaching forest has nearly reclaimed this section of road as its own and the smell of grass and loam fills your nostrils. Perhaps a hint of wood- smoke is mingled in there also, but it is hard to be sure. The road continues to the north and east, and is blocked by thick bushes to the south and west.

2827.name: Roof of the Jail
2827.description: It was very convenient of the Burghers of the city to build the jail right next to the thieves' guild. A skylight protrudes from the tarred roof, and a nice penthouse sits on the roof to the east.

2830.name: West Goldfish Lane
2830.description: A triangular building defines how the streets intersect here. It is home to Manfred's Fruits, a nice place to go to prevent scurvy.

2831.name: Manfreds Fruits
2831.description: You can buy seasonal fruits and vegetables in this small, triangular room. A trapdoor leads to the cellar where Manfred keeps his overstock.

npcs.name: Manfred the Fruited
npcs.description: He has orange hair.  It covers his entire body, including the knuckles of his

2832.name: Niche Circle
2832.description: This street runs behind Manfred's Fruits. To the south is a very large, unmarked building in good repair, but there seems to be no way in. You think that you see movement in one of the upstairs windows. You get the uncomfortable feeling that you are being watched.

2502.name: Guild Rooftop
2502.description: The roof is littered with the discarded ashes of pipeweed smokers. Little bits of lint and string also cover the surface of the building. Nearby is a small penthouse with the guild logo carved in the door.

2503.name: Penthouse Lounge
2503.description: A small bar is at one end of this lounge. The place would be a lot more comfortable if someone wouldn't keep stealing the cushions. Doors lead east and west, and a staircase made for small, nimble feet leads downward.
